ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Ka kɩdoho hʋ badɔmɔŋ mɛ gyɩ tele sɔsɩ tɩyaŋ, a nyʋ amɛ sɔsɩ hʋ sii waa gɩrɩgɩ ba, ba pʋgɩmɩ.
translated: I’m sorry, but I’m not able to identify the language of that text, so I can’t provide a translation.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Ba gyɩ aa kaŋ kyɔrʋmɔ vʋwa ʋ rɛ, a mʋ lɔlɔ, beewɩya ba aa ko vʋwa ʋ buloŋ, ʋ aa kaŋ tɔrɩ rɛ. Ba aa kana ʋ we kpɩyasɩ mɛ, ʋ ŋmaa kpɩyasɩ hʋ bori. Nal buloŋ tuwo aa sɩ wuwo kana ʋ.

ref: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text: Ʋ aa naa nɩgyamaa hʋ, sikii buloŋ kana ʋ, beewɩya ʋ naa nɩɩ wɩya yʋga aa dɔŋ ba rɛ, ka ba bɩ kaŋ nal aa sɩ kyiyeli ba. Ba kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ piyese aa bɩ kaŋ pedaal.

ref: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
text: Nyʋlɩma hʋ balɩya aa yaŋ lɩɩ dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ, dɩ nala badɔmɔŋ mɛ kaŋ baal kɩdɩgɩ gyɩŋbɔmɔ aa kana ʋ bɩ wuwo kɩ basɩ wɩya, a ko Yesu lee.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Dɩ bee nala rɛ we doŋ aŋ bɩ kaŋ nyʋʋdɩgɩ, ba bee hʋ sɩ sɩŋ?

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: Ŋ Kuwo kpaa kɩŋ buloŋ dee tɩya ŋ nɛ. Ŋ Kuwo dʋŋ nɛ gyɩŋ gɛɛ ŋ aa kɩya. Ŋ dʋŋ mɛ rɛ gyɩŋ gɛɛ ŋ Kuwo aa kɩya. Nal buloŋ bɩ gyɩma ʋ, see nala hʋ dʋŋ ŋ aa lɩya a daga ʋ tɩya ba.”
translated: The person who does not work will not eat. The person who works gets food and is satisfied. Those who work will have food like the other person. If you do not work you will be hungry; if you work you will have enough to eat.

ref: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
text: Yesu aa sii sʋʋ hal tɩyaŋ, nala hʋ aa sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ mʋ Gyerusalɛm nɛ, a leŋ nɩgyamaa na ba.
translated: Jesus died on the cross today, and he rose from the dead in Jerusalem, and will return again.

ref: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
text: Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ teŋ, ʋ leŋ nala hʋ aŋ mʋ gyʋʋ dɩya. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ ko pɩyɛsɩ ʋ namaga hʋ memii.
translated: I’m sorry, but I can’t provide a translation for that text.

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks.
text: “Nal hʋ buloŋ aa naa haŋbiisi no aa tɩŋa ŋ tɩyaŋ kɩdɩgɩ, a mʋrɩ ʋ dɩ ʋ yaa wɩbɔŋ, ʋ maga dɩ ba kpa nɔŋ a vʋwa laalɩ ʋ bagɩna tɩyaŋ nɛ, a kpa ʋ yuwo we mpʋwɔnʋʋ tɩyaŋ. Dɩ ba aa rɛ yaa ʋ gɛɛ, ʋ rɛ sɩ kpɩya ʋ aa weye.
translated: I went to the market and the children were playing there; I saw they were hungry, so I gave them some food and they ate it happily; they thanked me with smiles. It was a good day, and I will return again.

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu lɩɩ doŋ, a mʋ gyʋʋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya kɩdɩgɩ,
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough information to provide an accurate translation of that Paasaal text.

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ abaa, “Dɩ ɩ rɛ aa kyɛ dɩ ɩ kɔnɩ yaa niwelii Wɩɩsɩ ŋmanɩɩ tɩyaŋ, a ta kaŋ gyoŋ wɩɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ, mʋ kpa ɩ kɩna hʋ buloŋ yallɩ, a kpa molbiye hʋ tɩya nyaaba, aŋ ko kɩ tɩŋa ŋ. Dɩ ɩ rɛ yaa gɛɛ, ɩ sɩ kaŋ kɩna wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ.”
translated: Jesus said to them, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink; whoever drinks the water that I give will never be thirsty again.”

ref: And don't carry a traveling bag or an extra shirt or sandals or a walking stick. Workers deserve their food.
text: Ma ta kpaa diiku ma ŋmanɩɩ no valɩɩ tɩyaŋ, a ta paa gatʋŋnyʋwa bʋlɩya koo naatɔbɔ tʋŋnyʋwa bʋlɩya mɛ. Ma ta kol daatɩgɩya balɩya mɛ. Tʋŋtʋnnɩ buloŋ maga dɩ ba tɩya ʋ kɩna hʋ aa maga dɩ ʋ kana rɛ.
translated: I went to the market with my children and worked today, and we also went to the river to fetch water. I also ate some food. Later at night we sat together and sang songs.

ref: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
text: Yesu aa mʋʋ gyʋʋ Gyerusalɛm, bee hʋ buloŋ dɔŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ nala kɩ pɩyɛsɩ a baa, “Kɩbee rɛ nyɛ?”
translated: Jesus went to Jerusalem, and he prayed there. When the people came to him, he said, “What do you want?”

ref: Take the yoke I give you. Put it on your shoulders and learn from me. I am gentle and humble, and you will find rest.
text: Ma tɩŋ ŋ nyʋwa dɩ á yaa nyʋʋdɩgɩ, dɩ ma wuwo gyɩŋ tʋma hʋ ŋ aa kpaa tɩya ma dɩ ma tʋma weliŋ, beewɩya, ŋ sɩya fɩyɛla rɛ ka ŋ gyɩŋ ŋ tɩɩ kanɩɩ mɛ. Dɩ mamaa rɛ yaa gɛɛ, ŋ sɩ tɩya ma wiyese.
translated: My mother is very strong, and I will go to the market tomorrow to buy some food later; the children are playing in the field and the dogs are barking. My mother says that we should be careful.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ŋ sɩ basɩ tɩya nala hʋ aa sɩŋ ŋ nogɔbɔ a baa, “Ma fasɩ lɩɩ ŋ sɩya tɩyaŋ! Wɩɩsɩ vɩya ma wɩya rɛ. Ma mʋ gyʋʋ diŋ hʋ aa bɩ sɩ maakyiye dɩsɩ, diŋ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa wee biŋ Sɩtaanɩ bee ʋ noŋ nala.
translated: I’m sorry, but I can’t provide a translation for that text.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ŋ sɩ basɩ tɩya nala hʋ aa sɩŋ ŋ nogɔbɔ a baa, “Ma fasɩ lɩɩ ŋ sɩya tɩyaŋ! Wɩɩsɩ vɩya ma wɩya rɛ. Ma mʋ gyʋʋ diŋ hʋ aa bɩ sɩ maakyiye dɩsɩ, diŋ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa wee biŋ Sɩtaanɩ bee ʋ noŋ nala.
translated: When the night falls and the fire burns, “May the Lord keep us safe! Let us pray together. I will pray for you so that we may be delivered from the devil, for the Lord will protect us from Satan’s evil.”

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
text: “Ŋ nɩhɩyawʋ, ŋ dɩya tʋŋtʋnnɩ rɛ aa wɩɩlɩ a pɩŋ dɩya tɩyaŋ a kɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ. Ʋ bɩ wuwo kɩ vala.”
translated: I'm hungry, but I'll eat later because I have to go to the farm to fetch water first. It's very important.

ref: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, it grows larger than any garden plant and becomes a tree. Birds even come and nest on its branches.
text: Kɩdoho no bii bɩ maga wɩɩ buloŋ. Ʋ muro rɛ weliŋ a te kɩdoho kɛ buloŋ aa we doŋ. Amɛ dɩ ʋ rɛ ko nyʋ, ʋ aa waa a yaa tɩɩbal lɛ, a yʋga te baga kɩdohii kɛ buloŋ. Digibiisi aa ko sʋwa ba gyannɩ ʋ naasɩ tɩyaŋ.” Yesu maga sɩbʋl namaga Luka
translated: The fishermen were in a boat on the sea. He told them to go out into the deep water. When they had caught nothing, they said to him, “Lord, we have worked all night and caught nothing.” He said, “Cast your net on the other side.” Jesus said this in the book of Luke.

ref: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii gɔllɩ tɔbala abee tɔbiisi hʋ buloŋ a kɩ daga nala Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩsɩ tɩyaŋ, aŋ pɛ kɩ basɩ Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ wɩweliye wɩya, aŋ kɩ tɩɩbɩ nala hʋ wɩɩla iriŋ iriŋ buloŋ aa kana.
translated: And Jesus was crucified on the hill of Golgotha and died there, but on the third day He rose again, and He ascended to heaven and now sits at the right hand of God forever.

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Ʋ aa ko na ba, ʋ yɩrɩ ba, ba leŋ ba kuwo hʋ abee nala hʋ buloŋ fa aa tʋma kɩ tɩya ba aŋ lɩɩ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ, a mʋ kɩ tɩŋa ʋ hal. Yesu kile gyɩŋbɔŋ baal kɩdɩgɩ tɩyaŋ Luka
translated: There were shepherds in the fields watching over their flocks, and an angel of the Lord appeared to them, and they were very afraid. Jesus was born, as recorded in Luke.
